content:
# IVX IA SENIOR DEVELOPER — FINAL 100% OPERATIONAL HARDENING, LIVE EXECUTION TEST, DEPLOYMENT AND VERIFICATION

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Close every remaining IVX IA Senior Developer issue and prove the production IVX Owner AI can independently inspect, modify, test, commit, push, deploy and verify code end to end.

DO NOT RETURN A PLAN ONLY.
DO NOT RETURN NARRATIVE CLAIMS.
DO NOT MARK VERIFIED WITHOUT LIVE PRODUCTION EVIDENCE.
DO NOT MODIFY CRM, members, investors, buyers, deals, payments or authentication data.
DO NOT EXPOSE CREDENTIAL VALUES.

==================================================
CURRENT CONFIRMED STATUS
==================================================

The core senior-developer runtime is reported operational:

- Owner development-task routing works
- Tool registry works
- Runtime variables validate
- Repository inspection works
- Commands execute with exit codes
- GitHub commit/push works
- Render deployment works
- Production SHA matches GitHub
- Live diagnostics work
- Business data remained unchanged

REMAINING ISSUES TO CLOSE:

1. Render environment-variable updates can overwrite existing variables.
2. Rork Git proxy does not correctly forward pushes to GitHub.
3. Duplicate Render deployments can be queued for the same SHA.
4. Production IVX Owner AI must independently execute a fresh task from the actual IVX chat.
5. Live Work must show real persisted execution events through completion.
6. Failure and recovery paths must be tested.
7. No advertised execution path may remain broken or silently bypassed.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — SAFE RENDER VARIABLE UPDATE
==================================================

Replace unsafe environment-variable writes with a safe merge workflow.

Required behavior:

1. Read the full existing Render variable set.
2. Preserve every existing variable.
3. Merge only the intended additions or updates.
4. Submit the complete merged set.
5. Read the variables again.
6. Confirm every required variable remains present and non-empty.
7. Never log secret values.
8. Abort if the initial variable read fails.
9. Abort if post-write validation detects a missing variable.
10. Record a trace ID and rollback instructions.

Add tests for:

- add one variable without deleting others
- update one variable without deleting others
- failed read prevents write
- failed post-write validation reports failure
- secret values remain redacted
- concurrent updates do not lose variables

==================================================
PHASE 2 — STANDARDIZE GITHUB EXECUTION PATH
==================================================

Audit both paths:

A. Rork Git proxy
B. Direct GitHub API/Git Data API

Choose one canonical production path.

If the Rork Git proxy is repairable:

- fix forwarding
- verify repository selection
- verify branch selection
- verify commit creation
- verify push
- verify remote SHA

If it remains unreliable:

- remove it from the active production path
- disable misleading fallback claims
- standardize on the proven GitHub API path
- document the canonical implementation
- add clear failure reporting

Required tests:

- repository read
- branch read
- file read
- controlled file update
- commit creation
- remote SHA verification
- unauthorized repository denial
- invalid token handling
- branch protection handling

No silent fallback is allowed. The final report must state which path is active.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — DEPLOYMENT DEDUPLICATION
==================================================

Prevent duplicate Render deployments for the same service, branch and SHA.

Implement:

- deployment lock
- idempotency key
- active-deployment lookup
- same-SHA detection
- queue deduplication
- safe retry behavior
- stale-lock timeout
- owner-visible status

Required behavior:

1. Before triggering deployment, check active/pending deployments.
2. If the same SHA is already building or live, do not create another deployment.
3. Return the existing deployment ID.
4. Allow a new deployment only for:
   - a different SHA
   - a failed previous deployment
   - an explicit owner-approved redeploy
5. Persist deployment intent and result.

Add tests for:

- same SHA requested twice
- same SHA requested during active build
- failed deployment retry
- different SHA deployment
- explicit redeploy
- stale lock recovery

==================================================
PHASE 4 — LIVE WORK RELIABILITY
==================================================

Prove Live Work is connected to real persisted worker events.

Required states:

RECEIVED
AUTHORIZED
VARIABLES_VALIDATING
VARIABLES_VALIDATED
INSPECTING
PATCHING
TESTING
COMMITTING
PUSHING
DEPLOYING
VERIFYING
VERIFIED

Failure states:

FAILED
BLOCKED
CANCELLED
TIMED_OUT

Each event must include:

- task ID
- trace ID
- timestamp
- state
- current operation
- safe result
- error code if applicable
- retryability

Verify:

- refresh does not lose progress
- app restart does not lose progress
- duplicate events are not displayed
- ordering is deterministic
- Cancel works
- timeout works
- retry resumes safely
- terminal states cannot revert to running

==================================================
PHASE 5 — FRESH PRODUCTION EXECUTION TEST
==================================================

Run this task from the actual production IVX Owner AI chat, not only from the Rork workspace.

Controlled task:

Add one harmless owner-only diagnostics field:

"ivxSeniorDeveloperFinalVerification": {
  "verified": true,
  "executionPath": "<canonical GitHub path>",
  "renderEnvSafeMerge": true,
  "deploymentDeduplication": true,
  "liveWorkPersistence": true,
  "commitSha": "<runtime SHA>",
  "checkedAt": "<server timestamp>"
}

Restrictions:

- no business-data changes
- no database migration
- no credential changes except controlled variable-update testing in an isolated or non-secret test variable
- no CRM/member/investor/buyer/deal/payment mutation
- no public exposure of diagnostics

The IVX Owner AI itself must:

1. Authenticate the owner.
2. Classify the request as a developer task.
3. Start a Live Work task.
4. Inspect the repository.
5. Identify the correct files.
6. Modify code.
7. Add automated tests.
8. Run lint.
9. Run typecheck.
10. Run unit tests.
11. Run integration tests.
12. Commit.
13. Push.
14. Trigger one deduplicated deployment.
15. Wait for completion.
16. Verify the production endpoint.
17. Return structured evidence in the IVX chat.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — FAILURE TESTS
==================================================

Test safe failure cases:

1. Missing non-critical optional variable
2. Invalid GitHub token in isolated test configuration
3. Invalid repository
4. Invalid Render service ID
5. Duplicate deployment request
6. Failing test fixture
7. Deployment timeout simulation
8. Unauthorized developer request
9. Cancelled owner task
10. Live Work reconnect during execution

Expected:

- exact safe error
- no fake success
- no fabricated commit
- no fabricated deployment ID
- no secret leakage
- correct FAILED/BLOCKED/CANCELLED/TIMED_OUT status
- trace ID
- retry guidance
- no business-data mutation

==================================================
PHASE 7 — SECURITY AND DATA SAFETY
==================================================

Before and after the final test, record:

- members count
- investors count
- buyers count
- deals count
- project videos count
- JV deals count

Expected difference:

ZERO

Verify:

- server secrets are not in Expo public variables
- no secret appears in logs
- no secret appears in chat
- no secret appears in Git diff
- owner-only diagnostics remain protected
- non-owner access returns 401/403
- environment-variable audit output is redacted

==================================================
PHASE 8 — FINAL REGRESSION
==================================================

After deployment test:

- Owner login
- Owner AI normal chat
- Owner AI developer task
- Live Work
- image attachment
- PDF attachment
- message persistence
- Main feed
- Reels
- Deal Detail
- Members read
- Investors read
- Buyers read
- Landing page
- health
- readiness
- diagnostics

Confirm:

- no crash
- no infinite loading
- no narrative-only fallback for owner development tasks
- no duplicate deployment
- no variable loss
- no Git push failure
- no P0/P1 regression

==================================================
MANDATORY FINAL REPORT
==================================================

TASK UNDERSTOOD:
[exact scope completed]

ORIGINAL REMAINING ISSUES:
1. Render variable overwrite risk
2. Broken Git proxy path
3. Duplicate deployment risk
4. Production IVX execution proof pending
5. Live Work reliability pending

100% VERIFIED RULE:
Use VERIFIED only when all are true:

- Render variables update safely without data loss
- one canonical GitHub path works end to end
- broken or misleading Git proxy path is repaired or removed
- duplicate Render deployments are prevented
- Live Work events are real and persistent
- production IVX Owner AI executes a fresh code task itself
- commands have exit codes
- tests pass
- commit and remote SHA match
- Render deploy ID exists
- deployed SHA matches GitHub
- live diagnostics confirm the new release
- failure paths behave honestly
- business-data counts remain unchanged
- zero P0/P1 issue remains
